name: LOW BACK PAIN
chapter: DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS OF SKULL AND SPINE DISORDERS
definition: FAILED BACK SURGERY SYNDROME signs of dysfunction and disability + pain and paresthesia following surgery constellation of signs + symptoms resulting from compressive lesion in lower lumbar

demographics.Frequency: in 40% of pediatric + adolescent population; structural causes in 12-26%

dominant_finding: no enhancement on early T1WI (appears enhanced ≥ 30 min post injection)
discriminating_features: no enhancement on early T1WI (appears enhanced ≥ 30 min post injection)
deceased marrow signal intensity on T1WI
contrast enhancement of disk + adjacent vertebral bodies
heterogeneous enhancement on early T1WI (maximum at about 5 min post

general: no enhancement on early T1WI (appears enhanced ≥ 30 min post injection)
deceased marrow signal intensity on T1WI
contrast enhancement of disk + adjacent vertebral bodies
heterogeneous enhancement on early T1WI (maximum at about 5 min post
adhesion of roots to wall of thecal sac
abnormal enhancement of thickened meninges + matted nerve roots
